footer.tagline: Solar mobility, engineering precision, and community impact.

hero.kicker: Defy the limits of the sun
hero.subtitle: Candelaria is an interdisciplinary student project that designs, builds, and validates a solar-powered competition vehicle to advance clean mobility in Latin America. It is created for students, researchers, sponsors, and communities that believe high-performance engineering can coexist with sustainability, education, and social impact.

highlights.title: What it is
highlights.body: A university-led solar mobility initiative that combines engineering, design, and strategy.

highlights.title: Who it serves
highlights.body: Built for students and partners who want real-world innovation with measurable climate impact.

highlights.title: Core focus
highlights.body: Efficiency, reliability, and leadership development for future sustainable transport ecosystems.

fallbackItems.title: Aerodynamic Optimization: Alpha Phase Completed
fallbackItems.body: We reduced the drag coefficient by 15% through the new reinforced polymer chassis. Wind tunnel trials exceeded every expectation.
fallbackItems.alt: Futuristic solar car prototype close up

fallbackItems.title: Stellar-G2 High Sensitivity Panels
fallbackItems.body: Second-generation solar cells were integrated successfully, capturing energy even under low-light conditions.
fallbackItems.alt: High tech solar panel engineering workspace

appreciation.title: Thank you for being part of the
appreciation.body: Every kilometer traveled is proof of the unwavering support of our community. We are building more than a vehicle; we are designing tomorrow.
appreciation.imageAlt: Team looking at a sunset over a solar project

cards.title: AI Brain
cards.body: Predictive energy management analyzes weather in real time to optimize electric motor consumption.

cards.title: Solar Capture
cards.body: Aerospace-grade panels integrated into the body capture every available photon even on cloudy days.

cards.title: Zero Resistance
cards.body: Ceramic bearings and low-resistance tires designed specifically for the Bridgestone World Solar Challenge.

cards.title: Composite Materials
cards.body: The monocoque chassis uses a carbon fiber and honeycomb core sandwich, achieving exceptional rigidity with minimal mass.

hero.subtitle: Our teams are organized to transform ideas into tested solutions, from concept and simulation to manufacturing, operations, and communication. Explore how each area contributes specific expertise to keep Candelaria technically strong, collaborative, and competition-ready.

team.fallbackSummary: This team contributes across the project to support the full solar car development cycle.
team.emptyBody: There are no registered members for this team at the moment.

catalog.name: Committee
catalog.summary: Coordinates the overall project vision and keeps all areas aligned around shared goals.
functions: Sets strategic objectives, technical priorities, and semester impact goals.
Aligns communication between teams and facilitates timely interdisciplinary decisions.
Represents Candelaria institutionally and strengthens academic and industry partnerships.

catalog.name: Human Resources
catalog.summary: Sustains team talent and culture to keep a healthy and effective working rhythm.
functions: Reduces operational friction by improving information flow, coordination, and internal follow-up.
Accelerates talent availability through clear profiles, agile selection, and structured onboarding.
Strengthens wellbeing, recognition, and social media presence to position Candelaria across campus.

catalog.name: Design
catalog.summary: Builds a coherent visual identity for the car, the initiative, and public communication.
functions: Defines and maintains Candelaria's official visual identity across every communication touchpoint.
Designs the vehicle exterior and cosmetic elements with aerodynamic and brand criteria.
Develops merchandise, products, and the official website to amplify reach and positioning.

catalog.name: Chassis
catalog.summary: Develops a lightweight, safe, and stable structure to maximize on-track performance.
functions: Optimizes surface area and validates vehicle aerodynamics to reduce drag.
Evaluates stability, rigidity, and weight through iterative simulation and technical testing.
Designs a robust structural architecture that meets safety and manufacturability criteria.

catalog.name: Cells
catalog.summary: Turns solar radiation into usable and reliable energy to extend vehicle autonomy.
functions: Analyzes energy and radiation to estimate solar performance across environmental scenarios.
Runs autonomy calculations and simulations to optimize vehicle energy strategy.
Leads electrical design and solar array manufacturing with efficiency and quality standards.

catalog.name: Logistics
catalog.summary: Manages resources, processes, and institutional coordination so the project runs continuously.
functions: Controls budget, petty cash, fundraising, and disbursements to ensure financial sustainability.
Plans procurement, inventory, and relationships with departments and institutional partners.
Coordinates areas, standardizes internal processes, and documents workflows for stronger execution.

catalog.name: Batteries
catalog.summary: Designs storage and traction systems for safe, efficient, and intelligent energy usage.
functions: Sizes the energy storage system to balance capacity, weight, and reliability.
Selects and synchronizes motors to maximize propulsion efficiency under different conditions.
Integrates sensors, communication, and safety monitoring for stable solar car operation.

publications.subtitle: This section documents Candelaria's technical progress, field milestones, and strategic decisions so our community can understand what we are building, why it matters, and how each phase of development evolves over time.
publications.detailSubtitle: Read the full publication, including context, objectives, and key outcomes from the team.
publications.emptyBody: You can insert one publication to validate the database connection and translation flow.

hero.subtitle: Candelaria is more than a race project: it is a long-term educational platform where students develop advanced technical skills, interdisciplinary leadership, and practical solutions for cleaner transportation and responsible energy use.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.body: To be a benchmark student initiative in Latin America in sustainable mobility and clean energy, recognized for technical excellence, interdisciplinary focus, and the ability to form young leaders committed to innovation, sustainability, and global change. Likewise, we aim to win the Bridgestone World Solar Challenge.

vision.title: Vision 2030
vision.body: To foster interdisciplinary training, collaboration, and innovation through electromobility and energy-transition projects, generating academic, social, and environmental impact while positioning the student community as an active force in building a sustainable future.

items.title: Orbital Expansion
items.body: Launch micro-grids across 15 new cities, targeting 500MW installed capacity.

items.title: Distribution AI
items.body: Integrate predictive load management to optimize consumption by 40%.

items.title: Net Zero Global
items.body: Achieve negative-carbon operations at continental scale.

items.title: Responsible innovation
items.body: We explore new technical solutions with ethical criteria, prioritizing safety, feasibility, and sustainable impact.

items.title: Interdisciplinary collaboration
items.body: We integrate engineering, design, business, and other fields to build stronger and more complete solutions.

items.title: Sustainability with real impact
items.body: We pursue measurable results in clean mobility, energy efficiency, and social transformation from the university context.

items.title: Academic rigor
items.body: We make decisions based on evidence, technical validation, and continuous learning at every stage.

items.title: Student leadership
items.body: We develop leaders who can mobilize teams, inspire communities, and propose solutions for global challenges.

paragraphs: Candelaria is an interdisciplinary student initiative born in 2022 at Universidad de los Andes, focused on electromobility, energy transition, and sustainable innovation.

The project emerged from a group of students motivated by the possibility of building a solar-powered vehicle for the World Solar Challenge, who realized their impact could go beyond a competition. Candelaria evolved into an academic and collaborative platform that seeks to lead dialogue, learning, and action around sustainable mobility inside and outside the university.

Candelaria is made up of students and graduates from multiple schools, engineering, design, business, and others, working together to develop real, creative, and technically solid solutions to current energy challenges.

history.subtitle: The path of an idea that became an engine of change.

items.title: Foundation (2021)
items.body: Candelaria was born in a garage lab with a mission to democratize solar energy through intelligent networks.

items.title: First Functional Prototype
items.body: We achieved the first successful community-scale wireless energy transfer.

items.title: Team Expansion
items.body: AI and orbital-network specialists joined, doubling our R&D and deployment capacity.

items.title: Current Milestones
items.body: Launch of molecular management platform and international market opening.

support.title: Do you want to support
support.body: At the moment, Candelaria project has only being sponsored by the Universidad de los Andes and the effort of its members. But if you are interested in sponsor or support the project with any help, we will be forever thankful with you.

hero.subtitle: Candelaria grows through a community that believes in student talent, scientific rigor, and sustainable innovation. Your support helps us prototype faster, validate safer designs, and open learning opportunities for the next generation of mobility leaders.

plans.title: Elite Memberships
plans.subtitle: Choose the support tier that best matches your commitment and join a partnership model designed to deliver visibility, collaboration, and measurable impact.

contribution.body: Every contribution, regardless of size, helps us fund components, testing sessions, and student training. Your trust directly accelerates safer prototypes, stronger performance, and a project that inspires more young people to build sustainable technology.
